qas.q: It is getting late on Friday. You are reviewing your employees annual self evaluation. Your comments are due on Monday. You can email your employees information to yourself so you can work on it this weekend and go home now. Which method would be the BEST way to send this information?
qas.a: Use the government email system so you can encrypt the information and open the email on your government issued laptop

qas.q: What should you do if someone asks to use your government issued mobile device (phone/laptop..etc)?
qas.a: Decline to lend your phone / laptop

qas.q: Where should you store PII / PHI?
qas.a: Information should be secured in a cabinet or container while not in use

qas.a: Taking classified documents from your workspace

qas.a: Ask them to verify their name and office number

qas.a: Senior government personnel, military or civilian

qas.a: It is inherently not a secure technology

qas.a: Delete email from senders you do not know

qas.a: As a best practice, labeling all classified removable media and considering all unlabeled removable media as unclassified

qas.q: What is NOT Personally Identifiable Information (PII)?
Of the following, which is NOT a method to protect sensitive information?
qas.a: After work hours, storing sensitive information in unlocked containers, desks, or cabinets if security is not present

qas.a: When using a public device with a card reader, only use your DoD CAC to access unclassified information

qas.a: is only allowed if the organization permits it

qas.a: Leave the cd where it is

qas.a: Unclassified information cleared for public release

qas.a: Validate all friend requests through another source before confirming them

qas.a: Monitor credit card statements for unauthorized purchases

What information relates to the physical or mental health of an individual?

qas.a: Make note of any identifying information and the website URL and report it to your security office

qas.a: Contact the recipient to confirm receipt

qas.a: Member of a religion or faith

qas.a: Remove security badge as you enter a restaurant or retail establishment

qas.q: ActiveX is a type of this?
Which of the following is NOT a security best practice when saving cookies to a hard drive?
qas.a: Looking for "https" in the URL. All https sites are legitimate.

qas.q: Which is NOT a requirement for telework?
qas.a: Telework is only authorized for unclassified and confidential information

qas.a: Request the user's full name and phone number

qas.a: Put the CD in the trash

qas.a: Using NIPRNet tokens on systems of higher classification level

"Spillage" occurs when
qas.a: Personal information is inadvertently posted at a website

The following practices help prevent viruses and the downloading of malicious code except.
qas.a: Scan external files from only unverifiable sources before uploading to computer
